---
name: nezam-cloudflare-edge
description: Cloudflare Workers, KV/D1/R2, Pages, cache rules, and geographic routing patterns at the edge.
version: 1.0.0

---
# Purpose

Specify Cloudflare edge architecture: Workers + bindings (KV, D1, R2, Queues, Durable Objects), Pages, cache rules, security (WAF, Bot, Turnstile), and geographic routing. Single-responsibility: edge platform contract.

# Inputs

- API contract and gateway policy from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-api-design/SKILL.md` and `@.cursor/skills/nezam-api-gateway/SKILL.md`.
- Cache strategy from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-cache-strategies/SKILL.md`.
- Secrets policy from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-secret-management/SKILL.md`.
- Workload region/latency constraints.

# Step-by-Step Workflow

1. Author `wrangler.jsonc` (preferred) per Worker; declare bindings: KV, D1, R2, Queues, Durable Objects, AI, Vectorize, Hyperdrive.
2. Apply Workers best practices: avoid global mutable state, await all promises (no floating), prefer streaming responses, log structured JSON.
3. Choose data store per use case: KV (eventual cache), D1 (relational), R2 (object), DO (stateful coordination).
4. Configure Cache Rules and Cache-Tags via Cache API; set `cf.cacheKey` overrides for variant control.
5. Security: WAF custom rules, Bot Management, Turnstile on forms, Rate Limiting Rules, mTLS for B2B.
6. Geographic routing: use `request.cf` properties (`country`, `colo`); deploy per-region logic without separate stacks.
7. Observability: Logpush to R2/S3, Workers Analytics Engine, Tail logs in CI for debugging.

# Validation & Metrics

- `wrangler deploy --dry-run` passes for every Worker before merge.
- p95 Worker CPU time < 50ms on hot paths.
- No Worker has global mutable state holding request data.
- All secrets stored via `wrangler secret put`; never in `wrangler.jsonc`.
- Logpush captures errors to durable storage.

# Output Format

- `wrangler.jsonc` per Worker.
- Bindings catalogue (binding name → resource → purpose).
- Cache rules table (path, behavior, TTL, tags).
- Edge security config (WAF, rate-limit, Bot, Turnstile).

# Integration Hooks

- `/DEPLOY` runs `wrangler deploy`.
- `/SCAN security perf` validates WAF + p95.
- Pairs with `@.cursor/skills/nezam-cache-strategies/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-cdn-optimization/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-api-gateway/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-secret-management/SKILL.md`.
- Honors `[.cursor/rules/workspace-orchestration.mdc](.cursor/rules/workspace-orchestration.mdc)`.

# Anti-Patterns

- Storing per-request state in module-level variables (cross-request pollution).
- Floating promises (work continues after response is returned).
- KV used as primary database (eventual consistency surprises).
- Embedding secrets in `wrangler.jsonc`.
- Calling fetch on the same Worker (recursive loop) without isolation.

# External Reference

- Cloudflare Workers docs (https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/) — current.
- Wrangler v3+ (https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/wrangler/) — current.
- Cloudflare Cache Rules (https://developers.cloudflare.com/cache/how-to/cache-rules/) — current.
- Cloudflare D1, KV, R2, Durable Objects docs — current.
- Closest skills.sh/official analog: cloudflare-workers / edge-platform.
